conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on extending Mandache's Theorem 1.1 to all abelian groups
summary: The conjecture that Mandache's lower bound $M(\delta) \geq m_{\mathrm{convex}}(\delta)$ could be extended to all abelian groups via Bohr sets was resolved by Aaron Berger in a paper submitted in September 2019, just months after the Fox–Sah–Sawhney–Stoner–Zhao preprint. Berger generalizes Mandache's popular-differences result for corners from finite fields $\mathbb{F}_p^n$ to all compact abelian groups (which includes all finite abelian groups) and to corners in $\mathbb{Z}^2$. The paper was published in the Mathematical Proceedings of the Cambridge Philosophical Society in 2021.

since_posted.title: Popular differences for corners in Abelian groups
since_posted.venue: Mathematical Proceedings of the Cambridge Philosophical Society
since_posted.claim: Extends Mandache's popular-differences lower bound for corners from finite fields to all compact abelian groups and to $\mathbb{Z}^2$, resolving the conjecture that a Bohr-set analogue would yield such an extension.

search_queries: Mandache corners lower bound abelian groups Bohr sets 1903.04863
triforce corners Fox Sah Sawhney Stoner Zhao corners abelian groups extension Mandache
Aaron Berger "Popular differences for corners in Abelian groups" arXiv MIT 2020
notes: Berger's paper (arXiv:1909.12350) was submitted September 26, 2019, a few months after the Fox et al. preprint (March 2019). It covers compact abelian groups (which includes all finite abelian groups via discrete topology) and $\mathbb{Z}^2$, matching the scope the conjecture had in mind. The Cambridge Core page confirms publication in volume 171, issue 1 (July 2021).
